verses.bsb: After Jesus had finished instructing His twelve disciples, He went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.

verses.kjv: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
verses.bsb: Meanwhile John heard in prison about the works of Christ, and he sent his disciples

verses.kjv: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
verses.bsb: to ask Him, “Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Go back and report to John what you hear and see:

verses.kjv: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
verses.bsb: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.

verses.kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
verses.bsb: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?

verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear fine clothing are found in kings’ palaces.

verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.
verses.bsb: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.

verses.kjv: For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
verses.bsb: This is the one about whom it is written:  ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has risen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.

verses.kjv: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
verses.bsb: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subject to violence, and the violent lay claim to it.

verses.kjv: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
verses.bsb: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.

verses.kjv: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.
verses.bsb: And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.

verses.kjv: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: He who has ears, let him hear.

verses.kjv: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
verses.bsb: To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:

verses.kjv: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
verses.bsb: ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’

verses.kjv: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.
verses.bsb: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’

verses.kjv: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.
verses.bsb: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is vindicated by her actions.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.

verses.kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.bsb: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.
verses.bsb: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.

verses.kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
verses.bsb: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus declared, “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.

verses.kjv: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.
verses.bsb: Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.

verses.kjv: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.
verses.bsb: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
verses.bsb: Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.

verses.kjv: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
verses.bsb: Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.

verses.kjv: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
verses.bsb: For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”

verses.kjv: AT that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.

verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, “Look, Your disciples are doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

verses.kjv: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
verses.bsb: He entered the house of God, and he and his companions ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for them to eat, but only for the priests.

verses.kjv: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
verses.bsb: Or haven’t you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and yet are innocent?

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.

verses.kjv: But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
verses.bsb: If only you had known the meaning of ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the innocent.

verses.kjv: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:
verses.bsb: Moving on from there, Jesus entered their synagogue,

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
verses.bsb: and a man with a withered hand was there. In order to accuse Jesus, they asked Him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
verses.bsb: He replied, “If one of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not take hold of it and lift it out?

verses.kjv: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
verses.bsb: How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored to full use, just like the other.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
verses.bsb: But the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill Jesus.

verses.kjv: But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
verses.bsb: Aware of this, Jesus withdrew from that place. Large crowds followed Him, and He healed them all,

verses.kjv: And charged them that they should not make him known:
verses.bsb: warning them not to make Him known.

verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:

verses.kjv: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: “Here is My Servant, whom I have chosen, My beloved, in whom My soul delights. I will put My Spirit on Him, and He will proclaim justice to the nations.

verses.kjv: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
verses.bsb: He will not quarrel or cry out; no one will hear His voice in the streets.

verses.kjv: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.
verses.bsb: A bruised reed He will not break, and a smoldering wick He will not extinguish, till He leads justice to victory.

verses.kjv: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.
verses.bsb: In His name the nations will put their hope.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
verses.bsb: Then a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed the man so that he could speak and see.

verses.kjv: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
verses.bsb: The crowds were astounded and asked, “Could this be the Son of David?”

verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
verses.bsb: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “Only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, does this man drive out demons.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
verses.bsb: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand.

verses.kjv: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
verses.bsb: If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?

verses.kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.
verses.bsb: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.

verses.kjv: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.
verses.bsb: But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

verses.kjv: Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
verses.bsb: Or again, how can anyone enter a strong man’s house and steal his possessions, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.

verses.kjv: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
verses.bsb: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.

verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.

verses.kjv: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
verses.bsb: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the one to come.

verses.kjv: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.
verses.bsb: Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.

verses.kjv: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
verses.bsb: You brood of vipers, how can you who are evil say anything good? For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.

verses.kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
verses.bsb: The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure, and the evil man brings evil things out of his evil store of treasure.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.

verses.kjv: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
verses.bsb: For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.
verses.bsb: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.”

verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

verses.kjv: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
verses.bsb: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

verses.kjv: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
verses.bsb: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.

verses.kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
verses.bsb: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.

verses.kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
verses.bsb: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.

verses.kjv: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.
verses.bsb: Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’ On its return, it finds the house vacant, swept clean, and put in order.

verses.kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
verses.bsb: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there; and the final plight of that man is worse than the first. So will it be with this wicked generation.”

verses.kjv: ¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, His mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to Him.

verses.kjv: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.
verses.bsb: Someone told Him, “Look, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to You.”

verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Who is My mother, and who are My brothers?”

verses.kjv: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
verses.bsb: Pointing to His disciples, He said, “Here are My mother and My brothers.

verses.kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.
verses.bsb: For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”

verses.kjv: THE same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
verses.bsb: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.

verses.kjv: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
verses.bsb: Such large crowds gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.

verses.kjv: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
verses.bsb: And He told them many things in parables, saying, “A farmer went out to sow his seed.

verses.kjv: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
verses.bsb: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.

verses.kjv: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
verses.bsb: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.

verses.kjv: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
verses.bsb: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.

verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
verses.bsb: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.

verses.kjv: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
verses.bsb: Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.

verses.kjv: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: He who has ears, let him hear.”

verses.kjv: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
verses.bsb: Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Why do You speak to the people in parables?”

verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
verses.bsb: He replied, “The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.

verses.kjv: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
verses.bsb: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

verses.kjv: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
verses.bsb: This is why I speak to them in parables:  ‘Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.’

verses.kjv: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
verses.bsb: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled:  ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.

verses.kjv: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
verses.bsb: For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.’

verses.kjv: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
verses.bsb: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.

verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
verses.bsb: For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.

verses.kjv: ¶ Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
verses.bsb: Consider, then, the parable of the sower:

verses.kjv: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
verses.bsb: When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.

verses.kjv: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
verses.bsb: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.

verses.kjv: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
verses.bsb: But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.

verses.kjv: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.
verses.bsb: The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

verses.kjv: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
verses.bsb: But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
verses.bsb: Jesus put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.

verses.kjv: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
verses.bsb: But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.

verses.kjv: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
verses.bsb: When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.

verses.kjv: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
verses.bsb: The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’

verses.kjv: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
verses.bsb: ‘An enemy did this,’ he replied.  So the servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’

verses.kjv: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
verses.bsb: ‘No,’ he said, ‘if you pull the weeds now, you might uproot the wheat with them.

verses.kjv: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
verses.bsb: Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat into my barn.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:
verses.bsb: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.

verses.kjv: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.
verses.bsb: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, yet it grows into the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and nest in its branches.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
verses.bsb: He told them still another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.”

verses.kjv: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:
verses.bsb: Jesus spoke all these things to the crowds in parables. He did not tell them anything without using a parable.

verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.
verses.bsb: So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet:  “I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden since the foundation of the world.”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus dismissed the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to Him and said, “Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.”

verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
verses.bsb: He replied, “The One who sows the good seed is the Son of Man.

verses.kjv: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;
verses.bsb: The field is the world, and the good seed represents the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one,

verses.kjv: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
verses.bsb: and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.

verses.kjv: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.
verses.bsb: As the weeds are collected and burned in the fire, so will it be at the end of the age.

verses.kjv: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;
verses.bsb: The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will weed out of His kingdom every cause of sin and all who practice lawlessness.

verses.kjv: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: And they will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.kjv: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father.  He who has ears, let him hear.

verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.
verses.bsb: The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and in his joy he went and sold all he had and bought that field.

verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:
verses.bsb: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.

verses.kjv: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.
verses.bsb: When he found one very precious pearl, he went away and sold all he had and bought it.

verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:
verses.bsb: Once again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was cast into the sea and caught all kinds of fish.

verses.kjv: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
verses.bsb: When it was full, the men pulled it ashore. Then they sat down and sorted the good fish into containers, but threw the bad away.

verses.kjv: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,
verses.bsb: So will it be at the end of the age: The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous,

verses.kjv: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: and throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
verses.bsb: Have you understood all these things?”  “Yes,” they answered.

verses.kjv: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.
verses.bsb: Then He told them, “For this reason, every scribe who has been discipled in the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings out of his storeroom new treasures as well as old.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had finished these parables, He withdrew from that place.

verses.kjv: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this wisdom, and these mighty works?
verses.bsb: Coming to His hometown, He taught the people in their synagogue, and they were astonished. “Where did this man get such wisdom and miraculous powers?” they asked.

verses.kjv: Is not this the carpenter’s son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?
verses.bsb: “Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t His mother’s name Mary, and aren’t His brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?

verses.kjv: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this man all these things?
verses.bsb: Aren’t all His sisters with us as well? Where then did this man get all these things?”

verses.kjv: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
verses.bsb: And they took offense at Him.  But Jesus said to them, “Only in his hometown and in his own household is a prophet without honor.”

verses.kjv: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.
verses.bsb: And He did not do many miracles there, because of their unbelief.

verses.kjv: AT that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,
verses.bsb: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the reports about Jesus

verses.kjv: And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
verses.bsb: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife.
verses.bsb: Now Herod had arrested John and bound him and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,

verses.kjv: For John said unto him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.
verses.bsb: because John had been telling him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”

verses.kjv: And when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.
verses.bsb: Although Herod wanted to kill John, he was afraid of the people, because they regarded John as a prophet.

verses.kjv: But when Herod’s birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.
verses.bsb: On Herod’s birthday, however, the daughter of Herodias danced before them and pleased Herod

verses.kjv: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.
verses.bsb: so much that he promised with an oath to give to her whatever she asked.

verses.kjv: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist’s head in a charger.
verses.bsb: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”

verses.kjv: And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath’s sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded it to be given her.
verses.bsb: The king was grieved, but because of his oaths and his guests, he ordered that her wish be granted

verses.kjv: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.
verses.bsb: and sent to have John beheaded in the prison.

verses.kjv: And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought it to her mother.
verses.bsb: John’s head was brought in on a platter and presented to the girl, who carried it to her mother.

verses.kjv: And his disciples came, and took up the body, and buried it, and went and told Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then John’s disciples came and took his body and buried it. And they went and informed Jesus.

verses.kjv: ¶ When Jesus heard of it, he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard thereof, they followed him on foot out of the cities.
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard about John, He withdrew by boat privately to a solitary place. But the crowds found out about it and followed Him on foot from the towns.

verses.kjv: And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.
verses.bsb: When He stepped ashore and saw a large crowd, He had compassion on them and healed their sick.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves victuals.
verses.bsb: When evening came, the disciples came to Him and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is already late. Dismiss the crowds so they can go to the villages and buy themselves some food.”

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.
verses.bsb: “They do not need to go away,” Jesus replied. “You give them something to eat.”

verses.kjv: And they say unto him, We have here but five loaves, and two fishes.
verses.bsb: “We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered.

verses.kjv: He said, Bring them hither to me.
verses.bsb: “Bring them here to Me,” Jesus said.

verses.kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
verses.bsb: And He directed the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, He spoke a blessing. Then He broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.

verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.
verses.bsb: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

verses.kjv: And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.
verses.bsb: About five thousand men were fed, in addition to women and children.

verses.kjv: ¶ And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
verses.bsb: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of Him to the other side, while He dismissed the crowds.

verses.kjv: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.
verses.bsb: After He had sent them away, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray. When evening came, He was there alone,

verses.kjv: But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.
verses.bsb: but the boat was already far from land, buffeted by the waves because the wind was against it.

verses.kjv: And in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went unto them, walking on the sea.
verses.bsb: During the fourth watch of the night, Jesus went out to them, walking on the sea.

verses.kjv: And when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit; and they cried out for fear.
verses.bsb: When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified. “It’s a ghost!” they said, and cried out in fear.

verses.kjv: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
verses.bsb: But Jesus spoke up at once: “Take courage! It is I. Do not be afraid.”

verses.kjv: And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.
verses.bsb: “Lord, if it is You,” Peter replied, “command me to come to You on the water.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
verses.bsb: “Come,” said Jesus.  Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water, and came toward Jesus.

verses.kjv: But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.
verses.bsb: But when he saw the strength of the wind, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, cried out, “Lord, save me!”

verses.kjv: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
verses.bsb: Immediately Jesus reached out His hand and took hold of Peter. “You of little faith,” He said, “why did you doubt?”

verses.kjv: And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.
verses.bsb: And when they had climbed back into the boat, the wind died down.

verses.kjv: Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.
verses.bsb: Then those who were in the boat worshiped Him, saying, “Truly You are the Son of God!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.
verses.bsb: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret.

verses.kjv: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;
verses.bsb: And when the men of that place recognized Jesus, they sent word to all the surrounding region. People brought all the sick to Him

verses.kjv: And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.
verses.bsb: and begged Him just to let them touch the fringe of His cloak. And all who touched Him were healed.

verses.kjv: THEN came to Jesus scribes and Pharisees, which were of Jerusalem, saying,
verses.bsb: Then some Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked,

verses.kjv: Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.
verses.bsb: “Why do Your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They do not wash their hands before they eat.”

verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “And why do you break the command of God for the sake of your tradition?

verses.kjv: For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
verses.bsb: For God said, ‘Honor your father and mother’ and ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’

verses.kjv: But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;
verses.bsb: But you say that if anyone says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever you would have received from me is a gift devoted to God,’

verses.kjv: And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.
verses.bsb: he need not honor his father or mother with it. Thus you nullify the word of God for the sake of your tradition.

verses.kjv: Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,
verses.bsb: You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied correctly about you:

verses.kjv: This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.
verses.bsb: ‘These people honor Me with their lips, but their hearts are far from Me.

verses.kjv: But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
verses.bsb: They worship Me in vain; they teach as doctrine the precepts of men.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he called the multitude, and said unto them, Hear, and understand:
verses.bsb: Jesus called the crowd to Him and said, “Listen and understand.

verses.kjv: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.
verses.bsb: A man is not defiled by what enters his mouth, but by what comes out of it.”

verses.kjv: Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?
verses.bsb: Then the disciples came to Him and said, “Are You aware that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this?”

verses.kjv: But he answered and said, Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Every plant that My heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by its roots.

verses.kjv: Let them alone: they be blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.
verses.bsb: Disregard them! They are blind guides. If a blind man leads a blind man, both will fall into a pit.”

verses.kjv: Then answered Peter and said unto him, Declare unto us this parable.
verses.bsb: Peter said to Him, “Explain this parable to us.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Are ye also yet without understanding?
verses.bsb: “Do you still not understand?” Jesus asked.

verses.kjv: Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?
verses.bsb: “Do you not yet realize that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then is eliminated?

verses.kjv: But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.
verses.bsb: But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart, and these things defile a man.

verses.kjv: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:
verses.bsb: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false testimony, and slander.

verses.kjv: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.
verses.bsb: These are what defile a man, but eating with unwashed hands does not defile him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.
verses.bsb: Leaving that place, Jesus withdrew to the district of Tyre and Sidon.

verses.kjv: And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.
verses.bsb: And a Canaanite woman from that region came to Him, crying out, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on me! My daughter is miserably possessed by a demon.”

verses.kjv: But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.
verses.bsb: But Jesus did not answer a word. So His disciples came and urged Him, “Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.”

verses.kjv: But he answered and said, I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
verses.bsb: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.kjv: Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.
verses.bsb: The woman came and knelt before Him. “Lord, help me!” she said.

verses.kjv: But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it to dogs.
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “It is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to the dogs.”

verses.kjv: And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.
verses.bsb: “Yes, Lord,” she said, “even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their master’s table.”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.
verses.bsb: “O woman,” Jesus answered, “your faith is great! Let it be done for you as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that very hour.

verses.kjv: And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
verses.bsb: Moving on from there, Jesus went along the Sea of Galilee. Then He went up on a mountain and sat down.

verses.kjv: And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus’ feet; and he healed them:
verses.bsb: Large crowds came to Him, bringing the lame, the blind, the crippled, the mute, and many others, and laid them at His feet, and He healed them.

verses.kjv: Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.
verses.bsb: The crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they glorified the God of Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called His disciples to Him and said, “I have compassion for this crowd, because they have already been with Me three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they may faint along the way.”

verses.kjv: And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?
verses.bsb: The disciples replied, “Where in this desolate place could we find enough bread to feed such a large crowd?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.
verses.bsb: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked.  “Seven,” they replied, “and a few small fish.”

verses.kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.
verses.bsb: And He instructed the crowd to sit down on the ground.

verses.kjv: And he took the seven loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
verses.bsb: Taking the seven loaves and the fish, He gave thanks and broke them. Then He gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.

verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full.
verses.bsb: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

verses.kjv: And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.
verses.bsb: A total of four thousand men were fed, in addition to women and children.

verses.kjv: And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had dismissed the crowds, He got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.

verses.kjv: THE Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees and Sadducees came and tested Jesus by asking Him to show them a sign from heaven.

verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.
verses.bsb: But He replied, “When evening comes, you say, ‘The weather will be fair, for the sky is red,’

verses.kjv: And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?
verses.bsb: and in the morning, ‘Today it will be stormy, for the sky is red and overcast.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but not the signs of the times.

verses.kjv: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.
verses.bsb: A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.” Then He left them and went away.

verses.kjv: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
verses.bsb: When they crossed to the other side, the disciples forgot to take bread.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
verses.bsb: “Watch out!” Jesus told them. “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

verses.kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.
verses.bsb: They discussed this among themselves and concluded, “It is because we did not bring any bread.”

verses.kjv: Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?
verses.bsb: Aware of their conversation, Jesus said, “You of little faith, why are you debating among yourselves about having no bread?

verses.kjv: Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
verses.bsb: Do you still not understand? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?

verses.kjv: Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
verses.bsb: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?

verses.kjv: How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?
verses.bsb: How do you not understand that I was not telling you about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

verses.kjv: Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
verses.bsb: Then they understood that He was not telling them to beware of the leaven used in bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

verses.kjv: ¶ When Jesus came into the coasts of Cæsarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
verses.bsb: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, He questioned His disciples: “Who do people say the Son of Man is?”

verses.kjv: And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.
verses.bsb: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”

verses.kjv: He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?
verses.bsb: “But what about you?” Jesus asked. “Who do you say I am?”

verses.kjv: And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For this was not revealed to you by flesh and blood, but by My Father in heaven.

verses.kjv: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.
verses.bsb: And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build My church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.

verses.kjv: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
verses.bsb: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”

verses.kjv: Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the Christ.
verses.bsb: Then He admonished the disciples not to tell anyone that He was the Christ.

verses.kjv: ¶ From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.
verses.bsb: From that time on Jesus began to show His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and that He must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.

verses.kjv: Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
verses.bsb: Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him. “Far be it from You, Lord!” he said. “This shall never happen to You!”

verses.kjv: But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.
verses.bsb: But Jesus turned and said to Peter, “Get behind Me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to Me. For you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told His disciples, “If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.

verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.
verses.bsb: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.

verses.kjv: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
verses.bsb: What will it profit a man if he gains the whole world, yet forfeits his soul? Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?

verses.kjv: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.
verses.bsb: For the Son of Man will come in His Father’s glory with His angels, and then He will repay each one according to what he has done.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom.”

verses.kjv: AND after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,
verses.bsb: After six days Jesus took with Him Peter, James, and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves.

verses.kjv: And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
verses.bsb: There He was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and His clothes became as white as the light.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there appeared unto them Moses and Elias talking with him.
verses.bsb: Suddenly Moses and Elijah appeared before them, talking with Jesus.

verses.kjv: Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
verses.bsb: Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If You wish, I will put up three shelters—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

verses.kjv: While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them: and behold a voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.
verses.bsb: While Peter was still speaking, a bright cloud enveloped them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to Him!”

verses.kjv: And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their face, and were sore afraid.
verses.bsb: When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown in terror.

verses.kjv: And Jesus came and touched them, and said, Arise, and be not afraid.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus came over and touched them. “Get up,” He said. “Do not be afraid.”

verses.kjv: And when they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no man, save Jesus only.
verses.bsb: And when they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.

verses.kjv: And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.
verses.bsb: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, “Do not tell anyone about this vision until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”

verses.kjv: And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first come?
verses.bsb: The disciples asked Him, “Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Elijah does indeed come, and he will restore all things.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but have done to him whatever they wished. In the same way, the Son of Man will suffer at their hands.”

verses.kjv: Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.
verses.bsb: Then the disciples understood that He was speaking to them about John the Baptist.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they were come to the multitude, there came to him a certain man, kneeling down to him, and saying,
verses.bsb: When they came to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus and knelt before Him.

verses.kjv: Lord, have mercy on my son: for he is lunatick, and sore vexed: for ofttimes he falleth into the fire, and oft into the water.
verses.bsb: “Lord, have mercy on my son,” he said. “He has seizures and is suffering terribly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.

verses.kjv: And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.
verses.bsb: I brought him to Your disciples, but they could not heal him.”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus answered and said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.
verses.bsb: “O unbelieving and perverse generation!” Jesus replied. “How long must I remain with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy here to Me.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him: and the child was cured from that very hour.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy, and he was healed from that moment.

verses.kjv: Then came the disciples to Jesus apart, and said, Why could not we cast him out?
verses.bsb: Afterward the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.
verses.bsb: “Because you have so little faith,” He answered. “For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:
verses.bsb: When they gathered together in Galilee, Jesus told them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.

verses.kjv: And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised again. And they were exceeding sorry.
verses.bsb: They will kill Him, and on the third day He will be raised to life.” And the disciples were deeply grieved.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they were come to Capernaum, they that received tribute money came to Peter, and said, Doth not your master pay tribute?
verses.bsb: After they had arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your Teacher pay the two drachmas?”

verses.kjv: He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying, What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?
verses.bsb: “Yes,” he answered.  When Peter entered the house, Jesus preempted him. “What do you think, Simon?” He asked. “From whom do the kings of the earth collect customs and taxes: from their own sons, or from others?”

verses.kjv: Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, Then are the children free.
verses.bsb: “From others,” Peter answered.  “Then the sons are exempt,” Jesus declared.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.
verses.bsb: “But so that we may not offend them, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a four-drachma coin. Take it and give it to them for My tax and yours.”

verses.kjv: AT the same time came the disciples unto Jesus, saying, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
verses.bsb: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Who then is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus called a little child unto him, and set him in the midst of them,
verses.bsb: Jesus invited a little child to stand among them.

verses.kjv: And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” He said, “unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

verses.kjv: And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me.
verses.bsb: And whoever welcomes a little child like this in My name welcomes Me.

verses.kjv: But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.
verses.bsb: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the depths of the sea.

verses.kjv: ¶ Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!
verses.bsb: Woe to the world for the causes of sin. These stumbling blocks must come, but woe to the man through whom they come!

verses.kjv: Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.
verses.bsb: If your hand or your foot causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.

verses.kjv: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.
verses.bsb: And if your eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.

verses.kjv: Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: See that you do not look down on any of these little ones. For I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of My Father in heaven.

verses.kjv: How think ye? if a man have an hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and goeth into the mountains, and seeketh that which is gone astray?
verses.bsb: What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep and one of them goes astray, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go out to search for the one that is lost?

verses.kjv: And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth more of that sheep, than of the ninety and nine which went not astray.
verses.bsb: And if he finds it, truly I tell you, he rejoices more over that one sheep than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.

verses.kjv: Even so it is not the will of your Father which is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.
verses.bsb: In the same way, your Father in heaven is not willing that any of these little ones should perish.

verses.kjv: ¶ Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.
verses.bsb: If your brother sins against you, go and confront him privately. If he listens to you, you have won your brother over.

verses.kjv: But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
verses.bsb: But if he will not listen, take one or two others along, so that ‘every matter may be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.’

verses.kjv: And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the church: but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.
verses.bsb: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, regard him as you would a pagan or a tax collector.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.

verses.kjv: Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: Again, I tell you truly that if two of you on the earth agree about anything you ask for, it will be done for you by My Father in heaven.

verses.kjv: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.
verses.bsb: For where two or three gather together in My name, there am I with them.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came Peter to him, and said, Lord, how oft shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? till seven times?
verses.bsb: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked, “Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother who sins against me? Up to seven times?”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until seventy times seven.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy-seven times!

verses.kjv: ¶ Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, which would take account of his servants.
verses.bsb: Because of this, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.

verses.kjv: And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, which owed him ten thousand talents.
verses.bsb: As he began the settlements, a debtor was brought to him owing ten thousand talents.

verses.kjv: But forasmuch as he had not to pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.
verses.bsb: Since the man was unable to pay, the master ordered that he be sold to pay his debt, along with his wife and children and everything he owned.

verses.kjv: The servant therefore fell down, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
verses.bsb: Then the servant fell on his knees before him. ‘Have patience with me,’ he begged, ‘and I will pay back everything.’

verses.kjv: Then the lord of that servant was moved with compassion, and loosed him, and forgave him the debt.
verses.bsb: His master had compassion on him, forgave his debt, and released him.

verses.kjv: But the same servant went out, and found one of his fellowservants, which owed him an hundred pence: and he laid hands on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that thou owest.
verses.bsb: But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii. He grabbed him and began to choke him, saying, ‘Pay back what you owe me!’

verses.kjv: And his fellowservant fell down at his feet, and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
verses.bsb: So his fellow servant fell down and begged him, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you back.’

verses.kjv: And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay the debt.
verses.bsb: But he refused. Instead, he went and had the man thrown into prison until he could pay his debt.

verses.kjv: So when his fellowservants saw what was done, they were very sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.
verses.bsb: When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were greatly distressed, and they went and recounted all of this to their master.

verses.kjv: Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou desiredst me:
verses.bsb: Then the master summoned him and declared, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave all your debt because you begged me.

verses.kjv: Shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellowservant, even as I had pity on thee?
verses.bsb: Shouldn’t you have had mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had on you?’

verses.kjv: And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him.
verses.bsb: In anger his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he should repay all that he owed.

verses.kjv: So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.
verses.bsb: That is how My heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother from your heart.”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judæa beyond Jordan;
verses.bsb: When Jesus had finished saying these things, He left Galilee and went into the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.

verses.kjv: And great multitudes followed him; and he healed them there.
verses.bsb: Large crowds followed Him, and He healed them there.

verses.kjv: ¶ The Pharisees also came unto him, tempting him, and saying unto him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?
verses.bsb: Then some Pharisees came and tested Him by asking, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?”

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, Have ye not read, that he which made them at the beginning made them male and female,
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “Have you not read that from the beginning the Creator ‘made them male and female,’

verses.kjv: And said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife: and they twain shall be one flesh?
verses.bsb: and said, ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh’?

verses.kjv: Wherefore they are no more twain, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
verses.bsb: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate.”

verses.kjv: They say unto him, Why did Moses then command to give a writing of divorcement, and to put her away?
verses.bsb: “Why then,” they asked, “did Moses order a man to give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?”

verses.kjv: He saith unto them, Moses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because of your hardness of heart; but it was not this way from the beginning.

verses.kjv: And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except it be for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery.
verses.bsb: Now I tell you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another woman, commits adultery.”

verses.kjv: ¶ His disciples say unto him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marry.
verses.bsb: His disciples said to Him, “If this is the case between a man and his wife, it is better not to marry.”

verses.kjv: But he said unto them, All men cannot receive this saying, save they to whom it is given.
verses.bsb: “Not everyone can accept this word,” He replied, “but only those to whom it has been given.

verses.kjv: For there are some eunuchs, which were so born from their mother’s womb: and there are some eunuchs, which were made eunuchs of men: and there be eunuchs, which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven’s sake. He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.
